name: Henry Mancini
abstract: Henry Mancini ( man-SEE-nee; born Enrico Nicola Mancini; April 16, 1924 – June 14, 1994) was an American composer, conductor, arranger, pianist and flutist, best known as a composer of film and television scores. Often cited as one of the greatest composers in the history of film, he won four Academy Awards, a Golden Globe, and twenty Gr…

about_quotes.speaker: George Benson
about_quotes.evidence: He said, “George, it’s late. You’re going to have a cup of coffee before you drive that home.” So I went in his house, and he put on a record, Charlie Parker with strings. about_quotes.speaker: Joe LaBarbera
about_quotes.evidence: Henry Mancini is mentioned by the Interviewer in a story about James Moody; the Interviewee does not mention Mancini or describe any personal interaction with him.
about_quotes.doc_title: Joe LaBarbera interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, July 15, 2021

about_quotes.speaker: Jon Weber
about_quotes.evidence: Henry Mancini of all people — I was playing a gig in a hotel, quite young, and he walks in... he said, 'Come here a second, how old are you?' I said, '25.'
about_quotes.doc_title: Jon Weber interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, June 2, 2012

about_quotes.speaker: Plas Johnson
about_quotes.evidence: I played on several things with him before that. I’d also played on his “Peter Gunn.” ... I guess that’s how I came to work on his “Pink Panther.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Plas Johnson interviewed by Monk Rowe, Sarasota, Florida, April 12, 1996

about_quotes.speaker: Onaje Allan Gumbs
about_quotes.evidence: Henry Mancini had an integral part in my development as a musician and a music lover.
about_quotes.doc_title: Onaje Allan Gumbs interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 12, 2001
